content.claim: Hungary's Education Minister Judit Lannert framed the 6- and 8-year grammar schools as mechanisms for elite self-separation rather than educational development, arguing that their students' performance advantage is primarily attributable to family background rather than institutional value-added.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: This is a stated interpretive claim by a government official, actively contested by opposition (Fidesz parliamentary group leader Gergely Gulyás)
No independent empirical comparison of value-added is provided in the source
The framing occurs in an active political dispute, not in settled or peer-reviewed analysis
The claim about family background's explanatory role requires causal decomposition analysis not cited in the notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
